title: Tephroseris flammea

This classification is based on its shared characteristics with other members of the Asteraceae family, such as its composite flower structure. Tephroseris flammea is also a part of the genus Tephroseris, which is a group of flowering plants closely related to the genus Cremanium. The genus Tephroseris is often placed within the tribe Senecioneae, which includes other similar species of flowering plants. This classification is subject to change as new research and discoveries are made in the field of botany.

intro: Tephroseris flammea, also known as Flaming Marigold or Autumn Daisy, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it beneficial for treating conditions such as arthritis and protecting against cell damage.
